title: ranunculus gregarius subsp. nevadensis
intro: Ranunculus gregarius subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Nevada buttercup or mountain crowfoot, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus gregarius plant, a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the nervous system and relieve inflammation. The health benefits of Ranunculus gregarius subsp. nevadensis include its potential to reduce fever, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism and arthritis. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared for herbal use by infusing the flowers or leaves in hot water to make a tea, or by drying and crushing the plant for use in capsules or tinctures.
